descriptions.text:
「リングにかけろ」は、同名の日本の漫画を基にした唯一のゲームであり、原作の「チャンピオンカーニバル」から「世界選手権」までをカバーしています。登場するボクサーは20人と2人の隠しキャラクターです。22人のボクサー全員に対して声は6種類しかなく、声優には坂口大助、稲田徹、神谷浩史、山田真一、伊藤あさこ、木佐貫敦が参加しています。

「リングにかけろ」は、プロボクシングではなくアマチュアボクシングのシミュレーションです。実際のボクシングと同様に、各ラウンドにはタイマーがあります。ゲームは漫画のスタイルを取り入れ、2人のファイターがさまざまなカットシーンを使ってアクションショットで戦う様子を描いています。各ボクサーには特別な技とスタミナメーターに加えてエネルギーメーターがあります。

Being the only game based on the Japanese manga of the same title, it covers from "The Champion Carnival" to "The World Championship" in the original. There are 20 boxers and two hidden characters. There were only six voices for all the 22 boxers, voice actors in this game include Daisuke Sakaguchi, Tetsu Inada, Hiroshi Kamiya, Shinichi Yamada, Ito Asako, and Atsushi Kisaichi.

Ring ni Kakero is a simulation of amateur boxing rather than professional boxing. Like in real boxing, there is a timer for each round. The game uses a manga outlook to the boxing scene as two fighters appear to fight using various cutscenes for action shots. Each boxer has a special move and an energy meter in addition to a stamina meter.

Ring ni Kakero (Japanese: リングにかけろ, Hepburn: Ringu ni Kakero; lit. 'Put It All in the Ring') is a Japanese manga series written and illustrated by Masami Kurumada. It was published in Weekly Shōnen Jump between January 1977 and October 1981. The individual chapters were compiled by Shueisha into twenty-five tankōbon volumes. A sequel, entitled Ring ni Kakero 2, was serialized in Super Jump between 2000 and 2008.
An anime adaptation produced by Toei Animation premiered in October 2004. It was followed by a second season premiered in April 2006, a third season in April 2010 and a fourth season in April 2011.
